Details: Removal of total microcystins and M-LR in water by combined treatment processes can be moderately to very effective, up to 100 percent removal. Due to the unique nature of each combined treatment process, performance must be evaluated on a case-by-case basis. Three references report on numerous full-scale monitoring efforts, however, typically only the raw waters and the finished waters were monitored. Processes included conventional treatment, chlorine, chloramine, chlorine dioxide, permanganate, softening, GAC, PAC and bank filtration. Most of these, reported for microcystins in the TDB, are fully or partially effective, therefore, effectiveness can not be attributed to specific treatment processes at these full-scale plants. However, in one study, pretreatment by bank filtration alone lowered M-LR concentrations by 61 percent.
